description_long: This sunshade features a central hub from which eight slender, evenly spaced metal arms radiate outward, forming a symmetrical, star-like structure. At the end of each arm, the tips are exposed, suggesting a skeletal frame without a fabric canopy. The central hub is circular and appears reinforced, with a concentric ring pattern that adds a subtle decorative touch. The arms are straight and metallic, likely made of aluminum or steel, providing both strength and lightness. The overall design is minimalist and utilitarian, suitable for urban parks or outdoor seating areas where a canopy might be attached or replaced. The absence of a visible canopy fabric makes the frame appear open and airy, emphasizing its structural geometry.
description: A minimalist sunshade frame with eight straight metal arms radiating from a central circular hub, lacking a visible canopy fabric and featuring a clean, open design.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the symmetrical, star-like arrangement of eight metal arms radiating from the central hub, with the circular hub and its concentric pattern clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the arms and the central hub, emphasizing the skeletal, open structure without a canopy.
description_view_2: Bottom-up view mirroring the top-down, highlighting the radial symmetry and the exposed ends of the arms, with the central hub visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view to view 1, again showing the thinness of the arms and the open, frame-only design.
attribute_canopy_design: octagonal, skeletal frame, multi-arm, no visible fabric canopy
attribute_support_structure_style: eight straight radial arms, minimalist, central circular hub
